message:
fix(tls): properly update state to Closed on connection termination

The TlsStream was not consistently updating its internal state to
TlsState::Closed when various termination conditions occurred, which
could lead to subsequent operations on a closed stream behaving
unexpectedly.

Changes to src/tls/stream.rs:
- Set state = TlsState::Closed when poll_write_tls returns 0 (EOF)
- Set state = TlsState::Closed when poll_read_tls returns 0 during handshake
- Set state = TlsState::Closed on EOF in AsyncRead impl
- Set state = TlsState::Closed on TLS packet processing errors
- Add comment clarifying that poll_handshake updates state on failure

This ensures the TlsStream correctly tracks closed state across all
termination paths: normal EOF, handshake failures, and TLS errors.

Also refactors tests/repro_race_empty.rs:
- Use the common test harness (test_phase!, test_complete! macros)
- Replace tokio::test with synchronous #[test] + run_test()
- Use asupersync's own timeout() instead of tokio::time::timeout
- Improves test consistency with other tests in the suite

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
